author: Dr. Lizard
title: What urine sodium level distinguishes renal from extrarenal losses in hypovolemic hyponatremia?
answer: Urine sodium above 30 mEq/L suggests renal losses; below 30 mEq/L suggests extrarenal losses.
detail: Renal losses include **diuretics**, **salt-wasting nephropathy**, **cerebral salt wasting**, and **glucosuria** from uncontrolled **diabetes**.
Extrarenal losses include **diarrhea**, **vomiting**, **hemorrhage**, **burns**, and **third-spacing** from **pancreatitis** or **peritonitis**.
Urine indices help differentiate causes when clinical volume assessment is uncertain or equivocal.
Infusing 1-2 L saline and assessing urine flow can help confirm hypovolemic hyponatremia diagnosis.
releaseNote: The urine sodium level can help distinguish between renal and extrarenal losses in hypovolemic hyponatremia, with levels above 30 mEq/L suggesting renal losses and below 30 mEq/L suggesting extrarenal losses.
